stepsTitle: How to convert PDF to PowerPoint
stepsSubtitle: Three steps. No headaches. Done in seconds.
step1Desc: Drag and drop your file onto the page, or click to open the file picker. No account needed — just pick your PDF and go.
step2Desc: Our converter processes your file on the spot. Each page in your PDF maps to a slide in the final PPTX — layout, images, and text included.
step3Desc: Once conversion is done, download your PowerPoint file. Open it in PowerPoint, Google Slides, or Keynote and start editing.
featuresSubtitle: Fast, private, and built for real-world use — not just demos.
feature1Desc: Every slide you get back is 100% editable. Click into any text box, swap out images, rearrange elements — it's your presentation to customize.
feature2Desc: Each page from your PDF becomes its own slide. The layout stays true to the original so you spend less time reformatting.
feature3Desc: Charts, diagrams, photos — they all transfer cleanly into your PPTX. No blurry exports or missing visuals.
feature4Desc: Most files are converted in under a minute, even longer PDFs. No waiting around, no queues.
feature5Desc: Your files are processed securely and deleted from our servers after conversion. We don't store or share your documents.
feature6Desc: Use it on your laptop, tablet, or phone. No app to install, no plugin required — just open your browser and convert.
whyConvertTitle: When does PDF to PowerPoint actually help?
whyConvertSubtitle: PDFs are great for sharing, but terrible for editing. If you've ever received a PDF and wished you could turn it into a real presentation, you know the struggle. Here's where converting makes life easier:
whyReason1: You received a report as a PDF but need to present it in a meeting
whyReason2: You want to update slides that were locked in a PDF
whyReason3: You're building a presentation from existing PDF content
whyReason4: A client sent you a brief as a PDF and you need it as editable slides
whyReason5: You're repurposing old presentations saved only as PDFs
whyReason6: You need to add your company branding to content you didn't originally create
conversionFlowCompat: Compatible with Microsoft PowerPoint, Google Slides, and Keynote
faqSubtitle: Honest answers about what our converter does — and doesn't do.
faq1Question: Is this PDF to PowerPoint converter really free?
faq1Answer: Yes, completely free. You don't need to create an account or enter a credit card. Just upload your PDF, convert it, and download your PPTX. No strings attached.
faq2Question: What happens to my files after conversion?
faq2Answer: Your files are automatically deleted from our servers after the conversion is complete. We process everything securely and don't store, read, or share your documents.
faq3Question: Will my images and diagrams be included in the PPTX?
faq3Answer: Yes. Images, charts, and diagrams from your PDF are carried over to the PowerPoint slides. The visual content is embedded directly into the PPTX file.
faq4Question: Can I edit the text in the converted PowerPoint?
faq4Answer: That depends on your source PDF. If the PDF contains real text (not scanned images), the text in the resulting PPTX will be selectable and editable. Scanned PDFs produce image-based slides where text isn't directly editable without OCR.
faq5Question: How many pages can I convert at once?
faq5Answer: You can upload PDFs up to 100 MB, and up to 10 files at a time. There's no hard cap on page count — longer documents just take a little more time to process.
faq6Question: Which programs can open the converted PPTX file?
faq6Answer: The output is a standard .pptx file, which works in Microsoft PowerPoint (2010 and later), Google Slides, Apple Keynote, LibreOffice Impress, and most other presentation tools.
